channel.title: Posts on keithkeekw@github
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on keithkeekw@github

item.title: Linux File Structure
item.description: Folder Name Description /bin /bin is the directory that contains binaries, that is, some of the applications and programs you can run. You will find the ls program mentioned above in this directory, as well as other basic tools for making and removing files and directories, moving them around, and so on. There are more bin directories in other parts of the file system tree, but we’ll be talking about those in a minute.

item.title: How to Use Crontab
item.description: Cron is a tool to configure scheduled tasks in Unix/Linux system. It is like Task Scheduler in Windows system. crontab is a command to edit all the scheduled tasks in the machine. Cron Table Format * * * * * Command_to_execute | | | | | | | | | Day of the Week ( 0 - 6 ) ( Sunday = 0 ) | | | | | | | Month ( 1 - 12 ) | | | | | Day of Month ( 1 - 31 ) | | | Hour ( 0 - 23 ) | Min ( 0 - 59 ) Specifying multiple values in a field

item.title: Hello World
item.description: First and foremost, welcome to my blog where I mostly will post things about my learning notes in subject like Linux and book reviews. There might some short snippet on stuff that I find very interesting to share and most will be around technology.

item.title: Today I Learned
item.description: Here’s a collection of information which resonate deeply in me. This list will consist all range of topics from self-developement, money management and many more. Updated: 28th November 2020 2020 November No. Title Link Category 1. Linux File System/Structure Explained YouTube.com Linux 2. Don’t Just Collect The Dots - Connect Them ChiefExecutive.net Productivity 3. Understanding Linux File Permission Linux.com Linux 4.

item.title: Now
item.description: Last Updated: 25 Nov 2020 I am trying… to work on this blog. to refresh my knowledge on Linux after a period of hiatus. to write as many scripts to automate my job as well as my life. Currently… configuring my vimrc file as I am started to learn about this powerful text editor. This page is inspired by David Winterbottom’s Now Page, which will lead you down a rabbit hole of other “Now” pages.

item.title: About
item.description: 👋 Hi there! I am Keith Keeand I am an Application Support Engineer in a Broadcast Management System company. Currently I am residing in Malaysia. I have been working in the IT industry for almost 14 years now and primarily on the customer support and project implementation. This blog is intented to share knowledge about technology especially in Linux and other interested project that I might wanna to share with the world.
